# Senior Frontend Interview Prep

> **516 interview questions** for senior/staff frontend loops — system design, React, micro-frontends, browser internals, and more.

| | |
|---|---|
| **Interactive guide (expandable UI)** | [https://panurag38.github.io/senior-frontend-interview-prep/](https://panurag38.github.io/senior-frontend-interview-prep/) |
| **Browse answers in this README** | Expand a section below, click a question — jumps to the answer on this page |

Each answer includes **Reasoning → Example → Trade-offs**, production examples (Netflix, Amazon, Flipkart, Myntra, Walmart, Instagram, X), and follow-up Q&A.

> **Note:** Anchor links work on the [repo homepage](https://github.com/panurag38/senior-frontend-interview-prep) or this README — not on `/tree/main` URLs.

---

## Answers


## System Design Scenarios (15) Full interview rounds

<a name="sysdesign-design-the-frontend-for-a-netflix-style-video-streaming-app"></a>

### Design the frontend for a Netflix-style video streaming app

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- Web only, smart TV apps, or both? (Assume web + TV web for this answer.)
- Do we need offline downloads on web? (Assume no — native apps handle offline.)
- Is DRM (Widevine) required for premium titles? (Yes for studio content.)
- Target regions and peak concurrent viewers? (Global, 50M MAU, evening peaks.)
- Personalization real-time or batch? (Hybrid — batch rows + real-time continue-watching.)

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Browse catalog: rows, search, genres, detail pages
- Video playback with adaptive bitrate, subtitles, audio tracks
- Continue watching, profiles, watchlist
- Recommendations on homepage
- Account settings, parental controls UI

*Non-functional:*
- LCP  {
  if (data.fatal) recoverOrShowError(data);
});
hls.loadSource(manifestUrl);
hls.attachMedia(videoRef.current);
```

**5.2 Homepage performance**

Virtualize horizontal rows (react-window). Image CDN with srcset widths 320/640/1280. staleTime 5min on catalog Query; prefetch next row on Intersection Observer. LCP element = hero banner — preload that image in SSR head.

```

```

**5.3 DRM (EME)**

Widevine via Encrypted Media Extensions — license server handshake opaque to FE except challenge/response proxy through BFF. Isolate DRM code in lazy chunk; fail gracefully with upgrade message on unsupported browsers.

```
// License flow via BFF — never expose raw keys in client
```

**Reliability**

**6. Reliability, failure modes & edge cases**- Manifest 404 → retry with exponential backoff, fallback lower quality
- CDN segment failure → hls.js auto recovery; user sees brief buffering not black screen
- DRM license fail → clear error + support link, not infinite spinner
- Partial network (airplane mode toggle) → pause + offline banner
- Memory on TV devices → destroy hls instance on route leave; single player instance

**Tradeoff**

**7. Alternatives considered**- Full CSR SPA for browse — rejected: bad SEO, slow LCP on mobile
- Redux for entire catalog — rejected: memory + unnecessary; TanStack Query cache with pagination
- Autoplay previews everywhere — rejected: bandwidth cost; gate behind data-saver + feature flag

**Production**

**Production example — Netflix**
*Context:* Homepage rows + detail pages are SSR/ISR for SEO and fast LCP; the player is a lazy CSR island using MSE/HLS and Widevine EME for studio DRM.

*Why they chose this approach:* Split browse (server-rendered catalog) from playback (client-only video APIs) so LCP is not blocked by multi-MB player code.
- **Pros:** Browse pages score well on CWV while player stays feature-rich
- **Pros:** ABR + CDN segment delivery scales to global peaks
- **Pros:** Continue-watching syncs via lightweight API, not full page reload
- **Cons:** Two rendering models = more infra and hydration edge cases
- **Cons:** DRM + EME fail on unsupported browsers need graceful fallback
- **Cons:** Autoplay previews everywhere would crush mobile bandwidth — gated behind data-saver

**Metrics**

**8. Success metrics**- Homepage LCP, CLS (poster aspect-ratio)
- Playback: time-to-first-frame, rebuffer ratio, exit before start
- API: catalog p95 latency, error rate on /playback/start
- Business: start rate from detail page, completion rate

**Phased**

**9. Phased rollout**
V1: browse + basic player + continue watching. V2: recommendations + profiles. V3: DRM premium + TV-optimized focus management. V4: preview clips + advanced ABR analytics.

> **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "I optimized for perceived playback quality and homepage LCP by splitting browse SSR from lazy player, with Query cache for catalog and strict cleanup for streaming resources."

---

<a name="sysdesign-design-google-docs-style-collaborative-rich-text-editor"></a>

### Design Google Docs–style collaborative rich text editor

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- Real-time co-editing required or async comments enough? (Real-time required.)
- Max concurrent editors per doc? (Assume ~20, not 10k like Figma.)
- Offline editing required? (Yes — local-first with sync.)
- Rich paste from Word required? (Yes — major XSS/complexity source.)
- Version history UI in scope? (Read-only history list, not full legal hold.)

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Rich text: headings, lists, links, basic formatting
- Real-time multi-user cursors and selections
- Comments and suggestions (v2)
- Offline compose + sync on reconnect
- Export PDF/HTML

*Non-functional:*
- Latency op propagation  sanitizeAndInsert(event.clipboardData));
```

**Reliability**

**6. Reliability, failure modes & edge cases**- WS disconnect → queue local ops, show "Reconnecting…", merge on resume
- Conflict after long offline → CRDT auto-merge; if schema breaking, snapshot + migration
- Large paste → chunk insert off main thread or warn user
- Tab crash → recover from IndexedDB ydoc state on reload

**Tradeoff**

**7. Alternatives considered**- Last-write-wins — rejected: loses edits
- Lock paragraph editing — rejected: bad UX vs Google Docs
- contentEditable raw — rejected: broken cross-browser, accessibility nightmare

**Production**

**Production example — Instagram**
*Context:* Reels and Stories editors allow near-real-time draft sync for creators; public feed remains async (post, comment, like).

*Why they chose this approach:* Real-time CRDT/OT only where simultaneous editing is core; async comment threads elsewhere to cut WS cost.
- **Pros:** Lower server load than full Google Docs model on consumer scale
- **Pros:** Offline caption edits queue and merge on reconnect
- **Pros:** Paste sanitization blocks XSS from copied web content
- **Cons:** Not true multi-cursor Docs UX on all surfaces
- **Cons:** CRDT complexity in hiring and debugging
- **Cons:** Large paste still risks main-thread jank on low-end phones

**Metrics**

**8. Success metrics**- Sync latency p95
- Reconnect success rate
- Edit session crash rate
- Time to interactive on doc open

**Phased**

**9. Phased rollout**
V1: single-user editor + save. V2: realtime + cursors. V3: offline + comments. V4: suggest mode + history diff UI.

> **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "I chose CRDT over OT for simpler offline merge, with structured editor (Lexical) instead of contentEditable, and isolated presence from document sync."

---

<a name="sysdesign-design-e-commerce-checkout-cart-payment-confirmation"></a>

### Design e-commerce checkout (cart → payment → confirmation)

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- Guest checkout allowed? (Yes.)
- Payment methods: card, PayPal, BNPL? (Card via Stripe Elements + PayPal redirect.)
- Multi-currency? (Single currency MVP — USD.)
- Inventory held during checkout? (15-min reservation server-side.)
- Tax/shipping calculated when? (Shipping step + revalidate at payment.)

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Cart view/edit quantities
- Multi-step: shipping → payment → review → confirmation
- Apply promo codes
- Order confirmation + email trigger (server)
- Order history for logged-in users

*Non-functional:*
- Zero double-charges (idempotency)
- PCI: no raw card data touches our servers
- Checkout completable in  **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "Payment flows are pessimistic with idempotency; cart edits can be optimistic with inventory validation gates before charge."

<a name="sysdesign-design-a-real-time-fleet-tracking-dashboard-fleetpanda-at-scale"></a>

### Design a real-time fleet tracking dashboard (FleetPanda at scale)

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- How many vehicles? (10,000 active, 1Hz GPS.)
- Admin only or driver app too? (Both — separate apps, shared API.)
- Historical playback or live only? (Live + last 24h replay v2.)
- Map provider? (Leaflet + OSM tiles — no Google API key.)
- Alert rules in scope? (Geofence breach toast v2.)

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Live map with vehicle markers, clusters
- Filter by driver, vehicle, status
- List view + detail panel
- Driver: send GPS, complete deliveries
- Admin: assign orders, allocations

*Non-functional:*
- Map update latency  **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "Separate hot GPS path from cold CRUD data — external store + imperative map updates, bbox subscriptions."

<a name="sysdesign-design-a-company-wide-design-system-for-12-product-teams"></a>

### Design a company-wide design system for 12 product teams

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- React only or framework-agnostic tokens too? (React components + CSS variables tokens.)
- Theming: light/dark + brand per product? (Shared tokens, product overrides allowed.)
- Release cadence? (Bi-weekly minor, semver breaking quarterly max.)
- Accessibility target? (WCAG 2.2 AA on all primitives.)

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Tokens: color, spacing, typography, motion
- Primitives: Button, Input, Select, Modal, Table
- Patterns docs in Storybook
- Codemods for breaking changes
- eslint a11y rules shared

*Non-functional:*
- Tree-shakeable — pay only for imports used
- Bundle impact  **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "Platform DS succeeds with semver discipline, direct imports, and visual regression — not a centralized gate for every UI tweak."

---

<a name="sysdesign-design-multi-tenant-saas-auth-acme-vs-globex-on-same-app"></a>

### Design multi-tenant SaaS auth (ACME vs Globex on same app)

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- Subdomain per tenant (acme.app.com) or path (/t/acme)? (Subdomain — cleaner cookie isolation.)
- SSO per tenant required? (Optional OIDC per tenant config.)
- Data isolation model? (Server RLS — FE never enforces security alone.)
- Custom branding per tenant? (CSS variables from tenant config.)

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Login/logout
- SSO redirect per tenant
- Role-based UI (admin vs member)
- Tenant-branded login page
- Invite users

*Non-functional:*
- No cross-tenant data leak in UI
- Tenant config load  **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "Tenant isolation is enforced server-side; frontend handles branding, feature visibility, and safe session UX."

---

<a name="sysdesign-design-offline-first-field-inspection-app"></a>

### Design offline-first field inspection app

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- Inspectors offline 40% of day?
- Photo attachments size?
- Compliance: edits auditable?
- Conflict if two inspectors same asset?

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Inspection forms
- Photo capture
- Sync queue
- Conflict UI
- Offline indicator

*Non-functional:*
- Zero data loss on crash
- Sync within 5min of connectivity
- Works on 3-year-old Android tablets

**Arch**

**3. High-level architecture (draw while talking — 5 min)**
SW caches app shell + form schemas. Reads from IndexedDB cache. Writes → outbox table. Background Sync / manual drain on online.

**Data**

**4. Data model & key APIs**OutboxEntry { id, type, payload, status, retries }

**Deep**

**5. Deep dives (interviewer picks 1–2)**
**5.1 Outbox pattern**

FIFO retry with exponential backoff. Idempotent server IDs.

```
await db.outbox.add({ type: 'SUBMIT', payload });
```

**Reliability**

**6. Reliability, failure modes & edge cases**- Conflict 409 → merge UI
- Quota exceeded → warn user
- Partial photo upload → resume

**Tradeoff**

**7. Alternatives considered**- Online-only — rejected
- localStorage queue — rejected: sync blocking

**Production**

**Production example — Walmart**
*Context:* Store associates use tablets for inventory checks in backrooms with spotty Wi‑Fi — forms and scans queue offline.

*Why they chose this approach:* Service Worker app shell + IndexedDB outbox; background sync drains when online.
- **Pros:** Zero lost scans during connectivity blips
- **Pros:** Auditable outbox retries for compliance
- **Pros:** Clear offline indicator sets associate expectations
- **Cons:** Conflict UI when two associates edit same SKU
- **Cons:** IndexedDB quota on old tablets
- **Cons:** SW update bugs can cache broken JS — version caches on deploy

**Metrics**

**8. Success metrics**- Sync success rate
- Queue depth p95
- Conflict rate

**Phased**

**9. Phased rollout**
V1 read-only offline. V2 write queue. V3 conflict merge.

> **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "Offline-first means outbox + idempotent API — not just Service Worker cache."

---

<a name="sysdesign-migrate-500-route-webpack-app-to-vite-strangler"></a>

### Migrate 500-route Webpack app to Vite (strangler)

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- Timeline?
- Can run dual build?
- SSR required?
- Test coverage level?

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Dev HMR faster
- Prod parity
- CI green
- No user-facing URL change

*Non-functional:*
- Dev server start  **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "Strangler with metrics proves parity before decommissioning legacy bundler."

---

<a name="sysdesign-global-notification-center"></a>

### Global notification center

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- Realtime required?
- Email/push in scope for FE?
- MFE shell?
- Read/unread sync across devices?

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Bell icon + unread count
- Notification list
- Mark read
- Preferences page

*Non-functional:*
- Unread count accurate within 5s
- List scroll 10k notifications virtualized

**Arch**

**3. High-level architecture (draw while talking — 5 min)**
SSE or WS to shell. TanStack Query cache notifications. Optimistic mark-read. Shell badge in header shared across MFEs via event bus or shared QueryClient.

**Data**

**4. Data model & key APIs**GET /notifications?cursor= — PATCH /notifications/:id/read

**Deep**

**5. Deep dives (interviewer picks 1–2)**
**5.1 Realtime invalidation**

WS event invalidates Query key ["notifications"]. Badge derived from Query data.

```
queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['notifications'] });
```

**Reliability**

**6. Reliability, failure modes & edge cases**- WS down → poll 60s fallback
- Optimistic read rollback on error

**Tradeoff**

**7. Alternatives considered**- Poll only — rejected for realtime UX
- Per-MFE bell — inconsistent count

**Production**

**Production example — Instagram**
*Context:* Bell icon in shell shows likes, comments, follows — unread count synced across feed, Reels, and DMs via shared QueryClient in meta app shell.

*Why they chose this approach:* SSE/WS invalidates TanStack Query ["notifications"]; optimistic mark-read with rollback.
- **Pros:** Single badge count across surfaces — no duplicate bells in MFEs
- **Pros:** Virtualized list handles 10k+ notifications
- **Pros:** Realtime feels instant without polling every 5s
- **Cons:** WS down requires poll fallback — badge may lag up to 60s
- **Cons:** Optimistic read can flash wrong count on error
- **Cons:** Shell owns notification API — remotes must not duplicate

**Metrics**

**8. Success metrics**- Delivery latency
- Read sync error rate

**Phased**

**9. Phased rollout**
V1 in-app list. V2 realtime. V3 preferences + push opt-in.

> **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "Shell owns notification surface; remotes emit events, not duplicate bells."

---

<a name="sysdesign-typeahead-search-at-amazon-scale"></a>

### Typeahead search at Amazon scale

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- Catalog size?
- Personalized results?
- Typo tolerance server-side?
- Categories in suggestions?

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Typeahead dropdown
- Keyboard nav
- Recent searches
- Empty state

*Non-functional:*
- p95  **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "Debounce + abort + skeleton — protect INP while feeling instant."

---

<a name="sysdesign-a-b-testing-platform-integration"></a>

### A/B testing platform integration

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- SSR?
- Sticky assignment?
- Expose variant to analytics?
- How many concurrent experiments?

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Assign variant
- Render variant UI
- Track exposure + conversion

*Non-functional:*
- No flash of wrong variant (FOVC)
- Same variant SSR/client

**Arch**

**3. High-level architecture (draw while talking — 5 min)**
Edge assigns variant cookie before HTML. Inline bootstrap flags in head. Components branch on flag. Analytics event on exposure once.

**Data**

**4. Data model & key APIs**bootstrap: { experiments: { checkoutV2: 'control'|'treatment' } }

**Deep**

**5. Deep dives (interviewer picks 1–2)**
**5.1 SSR consistency**

Server reads same cookie as client. Mismatch causes hydration bug — test both paths.

```
if (flags.checkoutV2 === 'treatment') return ;
```

**Reliability**

**6. Reliability, failure modes & edge cases**- Flag service down → default control
- Stale flag → TTL refresh

**Tradeoff**

**7. Alternatives considered**- Client-only assignment — FOVC
- Per-render random — invalid experiment

**Production**

**Production example — Myntra**
*Context:* A/B tests on PLP layout, checkout CTA color, and recommendation row order — assignment before first paint to avoid FOVC.

*Why they chose this approach:* Edge cookie assigns variant; inline bootstrap in HTML head; SSR and client read same flag.
- **Pros:** No flash of control checkout on treatment users
- **Pros:** Exposure event fired once for clean experiment analysis
- **Pros:** Sticky assignment across sessions
- **Cons:** Hydration mismatch if server/client flag diverge — must test both paths
- **Cons:** Too many concurrent experiments → sample ratio mismatch (SRM)
- **Cons:** Flag service outage defaults to control — may hide winning variant

**Metrics**

**8. Success metrics**- SRM check sample ratio
- Conversion lift
- Hydration error rate

**Phased**

**9. Phased rollout**
V1 edge cookie. V2 analytics integration. V3 self-serve experiment UI.

> **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "Assign before paint; measure exposure and conversion with same userId."

---

<a name="sysdesign-admin-analytics-dashboard-10m-rows"></a>

### Admin analytics dashboard (10M rows)

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- Real-time or batch?
- Export required?
- Drill-down depth?
- Role-based metrics?

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Charts time-series
- Filters date/region
- Table drill-down
- CSV export async

*Non-functional:*
- Initial dashboard  **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "Clarify data volume upfront — aggregate on server, visualize buckets on client."

<a name="sysdesign-social-feed-infinite-scroll-media"></a>

### Social feed infinite scroll + media

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- Public or authenticated?
- Video autoplay policy?
- Edit/delete posts?
- Moderation UI?

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Infinite feed
- Like/comment
- Image/video
- Pull to refresh mobile

*Non-functional:*
- CLS  fetchFeed(pageParam) });
```

**Reliability**

**6. Reliability, failure modes & edge cases**- Duplicate posts on cursor overlap — dedupe by id
- Broken image → placeholder

**Tradeoff**

**7. Alternatives considered**- Offset pagination — unstable with inserts
- Load all — rejected

**Production**

**Production example — Instagram**
*Context:* Home feed infinite scroll with photos, Reels previews, and ads — cursor pagination, aspect-ratio placeholders, video only in viewport.

*Why they chose this approach:* useInfiniteQuery + Intersection Observer sentinel; dedupe by post id; prefetch next page on scroll proximity.
- **Pros:** Stable scroll with reserved aspect ratio — CLS  **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "Cursor pagination + reserved media aspect ratio — core feed scalability pattern."

<a name="sysdesign-frontend-platform-team-charter-50-devs"></a>

### Frontend platform team charter (50 devs)

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- Products web stack uniform?
- Existing CI?
- Pain points #1?
- Platform team size?

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Shared CI templates
- Design system
- Deploy previews
- Observability SDK
- Docs

*Non-functional:*
- Reduce build times 50%
- Onboard new dev  **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "Platform success = product teams ship faster with fewer incidents — measure DORA not lines of platform code."

<a name="sysdesign-production-white-screen-after-deploy-incident-response"></a>

### Production white screen after deploy — incident response

**Clarify**

**1. Clarifying questions (ask first — 2 min)**- Blast radius all users?
- MFE or monolith?
- Recent changes?
- Rollback available?

**Req**

**2. Requirements summary**
*Functional:*
- Restore service
- Identify root cause
- Communicate status
- Prevent recurrence

*Non-functional:*
- MTTR  1% triggers auto rollback

**Tradeoff**

**7. Alternatives considered**- Debug prod without rollback — rejected MTTR

**Production**

**Production example — Flipkart**
*Context:* Big Billion Days deploy — white screen after release traced to wrong base path on lazy chunks (404 on remoteEntry) + stale Service Worker.

*Why they chose this approach:* Rollback canary within 15 min; Sentry release diff; chunk 404 checklist; SW cache version bump on deploy.
- **Pros:** MTTR  **Tip:** **Closing statement:** "Rollback first, hypothesize second — white screen is P0; use Sentry + Network tab chunk 404 checklist."
